features.name: whitebox_module_in_design_selection
features.description: Code should include a design where a whitebox (boxed module) is part of the selected modules processed by proc_* passes. Whitebox modules are vendor-specific or simulation models kept as black boxes during synthesis, and the proc pass must iterate over all_selected_modules() to properly handle them alongside regular modules.

features.name: abc9_ops_invoking_proc
features.description: Code should include a synthesis flow where abc9_ops calls the proc pass on derived modules containing both regular and boxed (whitebox) modules. The fix requires calling 'proc -noopt' to skip opt_expr, preventing warnings about boxed modules being silently ignored.

features.name: proc_opt_expr_warns_on_boxes
features.description: Code should include a design with boxed modules where the proc pass's default call to opt_expr triggers a warning because opt_expr does not operate on whitebox modules but is called within a context expecting full module coverage. Using 'proc -noopt' bypasses this warning by skipping the opt_expr call entirely.

features.name: all_selected_modules_iteration_pattern
features.description: Code should include the correct iteration pattern over selected modules: using all_selected_modules() to enumerate both regular and boxed modules, rather than iterating only over selected_processes() which silently skips whitebox modules without warning.
